title: Crispy Thai Chicken Kebabs
description: Savor the herbal, crisp flavors of these Thai‑inspired chicken kebabs, finished in a tandoor for a smoky finish—perfect as a hangover cure and keto‑friendly treat.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor or grill to high heat (200°C). If using a tandoor, line the grates with parchment paper to prevent sticking.
In a bowl, whisk together coconut oil, lime juice, rose water, coriander, turmeric, chili flakes, salt, and pepper to form a thin sauce.
Toss the chicken cubes in the sauce until fully coated; let them marinate for 10 minutes at room temperature or refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
Thread marinated chicken onto skewers, spacing pieces to allow even cooking; optionally, thread a few slices of red onion or bell pepper between chicken pieces for color.
Place skewers on the preheated grill. Cook 3–4 minutes on each side until the chicken is charred and juices run clear; the internal temperature should reach 74°C.
Remove the kebabs from the grill and let them rest for 2 minutes to retain juices.
Transfer to a serving platter, sprinkle with chopped cilantro, and drizzle a light splash of rose water for extra aroma.
Serve hot with a side of cucumber salad or a low‑carb dipping sauce like lime‑coconut chutney.
